subject: High Life: it’s the Med – but not as you know it...
snippet: Plus, a Greek island just for you and a surprising alternative to the beach holiday. View email in browser Kanoni Beach in Kassiopi, located on the northeast coast of Corfu, Greece, linking to BA High
body_excerpt: http://click.crm.ba.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzN9ADIAAAAAAFiKImRN_nFUVuMH9TFZygvl-aLDH_BwvCr93jry-W0i0uhOLx51gfZYWnKfJm5kAcZ4ZICzwyiLVWUeiKk6_I5AdELlbyyZjx6D4ZQ <u>View email in browser</u> http://click.crm.ba.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzN9ADIAAAAAAFiKImRO5UEI7LkGHvS2QTL-cA-_6kNqS-C1iTWL03z9GTCILR3yrfRBH1MO4TmicvRYw5xzdZ_LJMgoDMHX74F0EpJVqeRV9R_WIZg David, could this be your best summer yet? Here's what most travellers miss: the Mediterranean's best summer spots aren't always the most famous ones. Beyond the postcard cities are often overlooked destinations still serving exceptional food, culture and value. Ready to discover where? http://click.crm.ba.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzN9ADIAAAAAAFiKImRPL_qzB59iQ5ozs8Tg83R8HitDQ-SfKpDGCY7nK6Vuyp__ZJYRaHkpfcMT7DSZ3o72d8cS3OBrQprq7Vple73gipmKWeCfzI0 Are these the Med's most underrated summer city breaks? Or perhaps you're dreaming of a Greek island, but overwhelmed by choice? Skip the research rabbit hole. We've done the work – narrowing down the hidden gems so you find exactly what you're looking for. http://click.crm.ba.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzN9ADIAAAAAAFiKImRQN-zTDXPRH4sVCWwWUotlvK5pUHFv7byJWW1To_ZxR-lLZCjJWbD_rCr3Cc9VlEFZbku9YQq91Bk1R9t9l3QzDM70jt9TS18 Quiz: which Greek island is your perfect match? And here's a plot twist: this summer, many travellers are ditching beaches altogether. The real story? Mountain destinations that offer cooler air, jaw-dropping views and a calm you won't find on the coast. Could a hillside holiday work for you? http://click.crm.ba.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzN9ADIAAAAAAFiKImRRz5PyvxWx6NgkXDJ3e_GYM040_AxvW8GqRTg96VJFLMhzm-ohCKxK2ILmfKjG0p-yK4Sq_cpXJWBLaC1_C4fGkZNLhhV5uIQ Summer in the mountains: the surprising alternative to beaches . P.S. Want to win tickets to Taste of London next month? http://click.crm.ba.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzN9ADIAAAAAAFiKImRSEsJDekChBEfXD57eyFlVCkx2XcEGEMfC4nq7IjQzVvgzfTMRxyN2DrioFL7lkZ5np22ZUZSA6swITP88tAf-4g-So58K2ps Enter our prize draw to be in with a chance. http://click.crm.ba.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzN9ADIAAAAAAFiKImRTurKgHjMpNgbW4sSo9sUjCInb8v6rIG7ni7Mqas6mBG1QrJAYomQOOkeXCdJ3yVE01HSbhOlXJUWIZqpidJJV7SKFEuMMDyM Get summer ready http://click.crm.ba.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzN9ADIAAAAAAFiKImRUZTJk0LcHz5Qx3zq11CmUYMZy3u90Zfc0tv1HLx-HxaX-UWfMF0uOjlUjCSYg0dP4IEgPbJPybWFFsjfu27AhlO59WN2O4N8 Revealed: hotels with unbelievable swim-up bars Forget queuing at the bar – these Mediterranean properties let you sip cocktails without leaving the pool. http://click.crm.ba.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzN9ADIAAAAAAFiKImRVstyfE4V14AXLVBlQIGN5As_ROwBg06NfUWClsSeJrQzsPAO4cTCB_8rMdMnO4GMljZPILGT8RzaIIQIYFRTQtA1m1T4-r-s Dive in Sponsored content http://click.crm.ba.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzN9ADIAAAAAAFiKImRWzDHkH5N1-3N_GzcujV7TIWez56-EwVyq3ewgFkGVvpSZoz8Zr4pBrClhSnGUOALd2ec5S6v0hZmnC2WrXBBtd6BUhnLo7G4 Love interior design? WOW!house at London's Design Centre, Chelsea Harbour, is the ultimate showhouse, with more than 20 rooms created by A-list designers. http://click.crm.ba.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzN9ADIAAAAAAFiKImRXRKNOmH0MBchSahmzwYBQ4VIkG3ai7y8NIgFHBKQqux01R0I2vwYo42hPwj5MPn2Jj87NUlYcGbetjFhcUJi7IC2VYXPpxFU Step inside http://click.crm.ba.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzN9ADIAAAAAAFiKImRYZzaMzV59lF9pFJiP5FhDaZZDdcqPSbzbiIuWbwwwEng8NExRyIiKw2xLVO6U1f14r_ZU1pbGjv9gHf2fsTpKOGl-JHFLHz8 City breaks where your money goes further Great food, gorgeous streets, lower prices. These underrated cities deliver everything you want from a summer break – for less. http://click.crm.ba.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzN9ADIAAAAAAFiKImRZEwSm7TWtgYtUFQpOTfoBLzHVgZpqYFy3GcMSWuQXbABjHfv1JfjVeEP0uKJIqgaZF6D7fWYVExi5nsDNpdhEH19HI_WMKsE Start exploring http://click.crm.ba.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NzN9ADIAAAAAAFiKImRaYxWOrslVXIG6PpBwwoKxHc0UvXZ-FQEyzAbbX5bTB2NIe8BFoQ07yHXmnW1wwQdogLwZXd35twE214x6Oi0YZRpZbvXUGi4 Can you identify the UK city? subject: Reading books is a symbiotic act
snippet: Intermezzo, Infinite Jest, Lord of the Rings, and well-loved books. (Issue #428) Reading books is a symbiotic act Intermezzo, Infinite Jest, Lord of the Rings, and well-loved books. (Issue #428) By The
body_excerpt: Intermezzo, Infinite Jest, Lord of the Rings, and well-loved books. (Issue #428) Reading books is a symbiotic act Intermezzo, Infinite Jest, Lord of the Rings, and well-loved books. (Issue #428) By The Medium Blog (https://medium.com/blog?source=email-623e23a7d13f-1778667173585-newsletter.v3-15f753907972------------------------6e092e14_e7dc_48c7_b48d_66a3acde1f67--------bf3f56c7deec) ∙ May 13, 2026 ∙ 5 min read ∙ View on Medium (https://medium.com/blog/7083bb54b8b3?source=email-623e23a7d13f-1778667173585-newsletter.v3-15f753907972------------------------6e092e14_e7dc_48c7_b48d_66a3acde1f67--------bf3f56c7deec) If writing - actual human writing! (https://medium.com/blog/ai-is-making-us-all-writers-395285833741) - is the most important skill of the A.I. age, then where does that put reading? In Why reading "The Decameron" is as useful as ever (https://medium.com/@nikolaus.correll/why-reading-the-decameron-is-as-useful-as-ever-06cf21b76452) , Nikolaus Correll (https://medium.com/u/8624c420e311) writes that while reading is increasingly important (reading is, after all, how we primarily interact with the internet and A.I.), the type of reading we are doing is actually quite shallow. What you're reading matters, too: [Artificial intelligence] compresses entire books into a page, entire arguments into a paragraph, entire traditions into bullet points…. why read the original when the essence can be extracted instantly? Or maybe there is something deeper hidden in these original texts? For the voracious readers on Medium, the relationship between them and the books they read is sacred. Let's explore those bonds. ··· Books have an extraordinary power to permanently affect us. Their impact on our psyche, whether they are nonfiction or fiction, cannot be underestimated. - Sarah Olson, Should Bookstores Carry Problematic Books? ··· How books imprint on us "From 2020 to 2023, I spent three years essentially pretending to read books," writes Patrick Daly (https://medium.com/u/5f0845291787) . Daly tried to pick up the habit of reading again (http://redirect.medium.systems/r-MfEuCXH7Im?source=email-623e23a7d13f-1778667173585-newsletter.v3-15f753907972-7083bb54b8b3-----------------------6e092e14_e7dc_48c7_b48d_66a3acde1f67--------bf3f56c7deec) in a fit of pandemic-induced self-improvement, but he found himself struggling. He picked up David Foster Wallace's Infinite Jest on the recommendation of a friend, which "beat him into submission." Rather than give up or "staring at the pages for an arbitrary amount of time," Daly decided to just keep going: Yes, any book that takes 1000+ pages to relay its contents is going to try your patience, but the type of patience I'm talking about has more to do with obscurity than just the passage of time - the type of patience that asks that you sit in discomfort and rewards you for doing so with eventual hard-won epiphanies, unceremoniously sliding you a single piece of information that suddenly clicks into place hours or days or weeks of reading, thinking, grappling, and ruminating you either thought was in vein or didn't realize was happening at all. That's the power of our relationship with reading. It's not a one way street; we both interpret and are interpreted. And that is why reading books, not just summaries, are going to be the support column of the AI future. ··· The day I was leaving for Monkey Bay, my father whispered into my ear, "Study very hard. I want you to be a doctor." I nodded my head in agreement, but deep down in my heart, I wanted to be a writer. - Nixon Mateulah in The Kalahari Review, To All the Books I've Loved Before ··· How we imprint ourselves on books A recent Stockholm book club meeting (https://medium.com/@spookytanuki/ode-to-fragile-masculinity-or-you-all-misunderstood-sally-rooney-let-me-explain-to-you-c525ac94023f) about Sally Rooney's Intermezzo had writer Lena Blomkvist (https://medium.com/u/659953143276) considering what we bring of ourselves to the books we read. "Book i
